result.stdout:
6502.Net, A .Net Cross Assembler for Several 8-Bit Microprocessors - .NET Global Tool (Zafiro fork)
Version 2.8 Build 1
USAGE:
General:
  6502.Net.exe -o output.bin inputfile.asm

  -a, --no-assembly                   Suppress assembled bytes from listing.
  -b, --enable-branch-always          Enable (pseudo) 'bra' for the 6502.
  -C, --case-sensitive                Treat all symbols as case-sensitive.
  --createconfig                      Create a config file from options.
  -c <arg>, --cpu=<arg>               Specify the target CPU and instruction set.
  --checksum                          Display checksum information on assembly.
  --config=<file>                     Load all settings from a configuration file.
  -D <arg>, --define=<arg>            Assign value to a global constant in <arg>.
  -d, --no-dissassembly               Suppress disassembly from assembly listing.
  --disassemble                       Disassemble input files to output disassembly file.
  --disassembly-start                 Provide the disassembly start address.
  --disassembly-offset                Provide the disassembly offset.
  --dsections=<sections>              Define one or more sections.
  --echo-each-pass                    ".echo" output on each pass.
  -e <file>, --error=<file>           Dump errors to <file>.
  -f <format>, --format=<format>      Specify binary output format.
  -I <path>, --include-path=<path>    Include search path.
  -l <arg>, --labels=<arg>            Output label definitions to <arg>.
  --labels-addresses-only             Only include addresses in label definitions.
  --vice-labels                       Output label listing to VICE debugger format.
  -L <file>, --list=<file>            Output listing to <file>.
  --long-addressing                   Support 24-bit (long) addressing mode.
  --no-highlighting                   Do not highlight causes of errors and warnings in source.
  -n, --no-stats                      Supress display of statistics from the assembly.
  -o <file>., --output=<file>.        Output assembly to <file>.
  --output-section                    Output the specified section only to object file.
  -p <offset>., --patch=<offset>.     Patch the output file at <offset>.
  -q, --quiet                         Assemble in quiet mode (no console).
  -r, --autosize-registers            Auto-size .A and .X/.Y registers in 65816 mode.
  --reset-pc-on-bank                  Reset the PC on '.bank' directive.
  -s, --no-source                     Suppress original source from listing.
  -t, --truncate-assembly             Truncate assembled bytes in listing.
  --verbose-asm                       Include all directives/comments in listing.
  -w, --no-warn                       Suppress all warnings.
  --Wall                              Enable all warnings.
  --Wcase-mismatch                    Warn on symbol case mismatch.
  --Werror                            Treat all warnings as errors.
  --Wjump-bug                         Warn about the JMP bug in 6502 mode.
  --Wleft                             Warn when a whitespace precedes a label.
  --Wno-unused-sections               Don't warn about unused sections.
  --wregister-as-identifier           Warn when a register is being used as an identifier.
  --Wsimplify-call-return             Warn when call and return can be simplified to jump.
  --Wtext-in-non-text-pseudo-ops      Warn about usage of text in pseudo-ops.
  --Wunreferenced-symbols             Warn about unreferenced symbols.
  --help                              Display this help screen.
  --version                           Display version information.
  <inputs>                            The source file(s) to assemble.

To log a defect, go to https://github.com/informedcitizenry/6502.Net/issues
